jokes.text: This is a joke shirt. 'Cause I'm a trainer.
jokes.setup: Jeremy is clearly wearing workout clothes to his apartment
jokes.punchline: He deadpans that it's a 'joke shirt' because he's a trainer, missing that his explanation makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy establishing Jeremy as oblivious. Basic but functional setup for his personality.

jokes.text: Like Shania... Twain.
jokes.setup: Jeremy claims he trains VIP clients at night
jokes.punchline: He names Shania Twain with a dramatic pause, an absurdly dated celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedic timing with the pause. The specificity of Shania Twain as a VIP client is perfectly absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: Okay, I love you too.
jokes.setup: Jeremy says 'I owe you one' casually
jokes.punchline: Abbi mishears and responds with 'I love you too' to someone she barely knows
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy showing Abbi's romantic desperation. The mishearing feels natural and creates genuine secondhand embarrassment.

jokes.text: All right, love you too.
jokes.setup: Abbi already said 'love you too' by mistake earlier
jokes.punchline: Jeremy accidentally says it back at the end, compounding the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation and callback that compounds the awkwardness. Shows both characters are equally flustered.

jokes.text: Were you just sleeping with your eyes open?
jokes.setup: Ilana appears to be awake at her desk
jokes.punchline: Her boss catches her in an impossible sleeping position
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about workplace slacking. Sets up Ilana's character as completely inappropriate employee.

jokes.text: Computer's not on. It's just idle, I think. It's not on.
jokes.setup: Ilana tries to excuse her lack of work
jokes.punchline: Boss flatly corrects her obvious lie about the computer
jokes.explanation: Solid workplace comedy showing Ilana's terrible excuses meeting reality. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Sophie's Choice.
jokes.setup: Ilana is covering up inappropriate clothing
jokes.punchline: Her shirt apparently says 'Sophie's Choice' - an inappropriate reference to a Holocaust film
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate shirt choice that perfectly encapsulates Ilana's oblivious boundary-crossing

jokes.text: But first, I'm going to take my top-o'-the-morning-dump-to-you.
jokes.setup: Boss assigns her to read the manual
jokes.punchline: Ilana creates an elaborate pun combining 'top of the morning' with bathroom needs
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that's very Ilana. A bit forced but the commitment to the elaborate pun works for her character.

jokes.text: A clump of my hair just fell out actually.
jokes.setup: Abbi is calling in sick to work with increasingly elaborate symptoms
jokes.punchline: The specific detail of hair falling out escalates her fake illness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of fake symptoms but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Run a quick 5K, sweat it out, then take an eight-minute nap. Wake up, do a shot of Echinacea, and then jump right into an ice bath. Immediately after that, suck on some ice chips, then urinate as hard and as fast as you can. Then eat a full onion, rub that onion underneath your armpits, any kind of gland, any kind of mucal cavity, okay? It's called cellular confusion.
jokes.setup: Abbi calls in sick to the gym
jokes.punchline: Trey gives increasingly bizarre and contradictory health advice culminating in 'cellular confusion'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly escalating absurdity with perfect pseudo-scientific conclusion. The specific contradictions (ice bath then ice chips) and 'cellular confusion' are comedy gold.

jokes.text: Join me in the showers. I'm not on my period.
jokes.setup: Trey talks to a female gym member
jokes.punchline: He inappropriately invites her to shower and mentions her period status
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Trey's inappropriate oversharing and boundary issues. Economical and character-specific cringe.

jokes.text: Popsicle incident. I totally forgot about that.
jokes.setup: Linda mentions why they haven't called Ilana for work
jokes.punchline: Ilana's casual dismissal of what was clearly a significant workplace disaster
jokes.explanation: Great setup that implies a disaster we never see. Ilana's casual response shows her complete lack of professional awareness.

jokes.text: Next thing you know, you're pregnant with his sperm and he's sanding down your headboard shirtless.
jokes.setup: Abbi explains Jeremy asking her to sign for a package
jokes.punchline: Ilana jumps to an absurdly specific domestic fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Ilana's sex-obsessed perspective. The specificity of 'pregnant with his sperm' and shirtless woodworking is hilariously detailed.

jokes.text: His dick, dude!
jokes.setup: Abbi mentions Jeremy said he 'owes her one'
jokes.punchline: Ilana immediately interprets this sexually in the crudest way possible
jokes.explanation: Pure Ilana character comedy. Maximum economy and impact, though not particularly original. Perfect delivery of her worldview.

jokes.text: You ate it like, as soon as you gave it to me.
jokes.setup: Ilana suggests Abbi wear the candy thong she got her
jokes.punchline: Abbi reveals she immediately ate the edible underwear
jokes.explanation: Great character beat showing Abbi's practical nature vs Ilana's sexual schemes. The casual admission is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Oh, I love that place. Yeah, it's usually good, right?
jokes.setup: Linda mentions the sushi place under Penn Station gave everyone food poisoning
jokes.punchline: Ilana enthusiastically endorses the restaurant that just hospitalized everyone
jokes.explanation: Good ironic comedy showing Ilana's questionable judgment. The casual endorsement of a poison restaurant is perfectly oblivious.

jokes.text: I said, 'Do whatever you want.' I am way too trusting of strangers.
jokes.setup: Linda's hair looks terrible
jokes.punchline: She gave a stranger complete creative control then immediately recognizes her poor judgment
jokes.explanation: Solid observational comedy about bad life choices. The immediate self-awareness adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I know you don't like me to answer the door because I don't technically live here.
jokes.setup: Abbi asks why Bevers didn't answer the door
jokes.punchline: He reveals his awkward living situation while showing he's been following weird rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bevers character comedy. Establishes his weird quasi-living situation and boundary issues in one line.

jokes.text: It's a flexible window. Windows are never flexible. That's not how they work.
jokes.setup: Delivery person explains the delivery window was 'flexible'
jokes.punchline: Abbi takes it literally and explains the physics of actual windows
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Abbi's literal-mindedness. Good economy and logic to the absurdity.

jokes.text: You'll never get off the island. What did you just say? I'm sorry. I'm watching 'Shutter Island.'
jokes.setup: Delivery person gives ominous warning about the island
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're just watching a movie, deflating the drama
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that builds then deflates tension perfectly. The movie choice is apt for the island setting.

jokes.text: You're a temp at a temp agency? Touche.
jokes.setup: Caller questions Ilana being a temp at a temp agency
jokes.punchline: Ilana acknowledges the meta-irony with 'touche'
jokes.explanation: Solid meta-humor about the workplace situation. Ilana's self-awareness adds to the joke.

jokes.text: I'm making bank, bitch! Oh, my God. You are insane. Ab, I'm becoming a mogul a la Jay-Z, a la Martha Stewart, a la Bethenny Frankel.
jokes.setup: Ilana gets one temp dog walking job
jokes.punchline: She immediately sees herself as a business mogul with increasingly absurd comparisons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ilana grandiosity. The escalating comparisons from Jay-Z to Bethenny Frankel show her delusions perfectly.

jokes.text: RiRi leans in for a kiss and then she... Goes down on you. Yeah. Yeah, you... you say that story a lot.
jokes.setup: Ilana tells elaborate fantasy about meeting celebrities
jokes.punchline: Abbi reveals this is a recurring story Ilana tells repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Ilana's repetitive fantasies. Abbi's weary familiarity adds authenticity to their friendship.

jokes.text: That's what white people do, that dog thing. Black people don't make out with dogs.
jokes.setup: Ilana suggests Lincoln is being standoffish with the dog
jokes.punchline: Lincoln matter-of-factly explains racial differences in dog affection
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational comedy that feels authentic to Lincoln's character. Good racial humor that doesn't punch down.

jokes.text: Nah, dude, it's a Fleshlight. Ilana, why do you have to ruin him for me? It's not a Fleshlight. It's healthy.
jokes.setup: Abbi speculates romantically about Jeremy's package contents
jokes.punchline: Ilana immediately assumes it's a sex toy and defends male masturbation
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Abbi's romantic delusions and Ilana's crude realism. The 'it's healthy' defense is characteristically Ilana.

jokes.text: There's General Tso, there's Dumbface. That's Ballsack, and that is star of 'Ugly Betty' and 'Who's the Boss?' The wonderful, the esteemed... Judith Light!
jokes.setup: Ilana names dogs based on their personalities
jokes.punchline: Goes from crude names to an elaborate celebrity impression introduction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from crude dog names to theatrical celebrity introduction. The Judith Light reference is perfectly random and specific.

jokes.text: Judith, now that I know that you are Judith Light, I can't let you back in that tiny-ass apartment.
jokes.setup: Ilana just named the dog Judith Light
jokes.punchline: She immediately treats the dog like an actual celebrity who deserves better
jokes.explanation: Perfect commitment to the bit. Ilana fully believes in her own celebrity naming and acts accordingly.

jokes.text: Jeremy's actually my... he's my husband.
jokes.setup: Abbi needs Jeremy's ID to get his package
jokes.punchline: She lies about being married to a neighbor she barely knows
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Abbi's desperation. The hesitation before 'husband' shows her discomfort with the lie.

jokes.text: Can you just like, look the other way this time?
jokes.setup: Garol won't bend the rules for the package
jokes.punchline: Abbi tries to bribe or charm her way out of following basic postal regulations
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy showing Abbi's escalating desperation, but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: 30 or like, 100. 30 or 100? These are for a guess-how-many contest!
jokes.setup: Linda discovers Ilana has been eating contest candy
jokes.punchline: Ilana's estimate range is absurdly wide and she's ruined a contest
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Ilana's complete lack of awareness. The wide estimate range shows she has no idea how much she ate.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I killed her! Judith Light!
jokes.setup: Ilana discovers one of the dogs (Judith Light) collapsed
jokes.punchline: She dramatically mourns the 'death' of her celebrity-named dog
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with genuine emotional stakes. Ilana's dramatic reaction to 'killing' a celebrity is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: If you do this for me right now, I'm gonna... I will buy you a Costco rotisserie chicken.
jokes.setup: Abbi needs Bevers to help with the package scheme
jokes.punchline: Her ultimate bribe is a specific grocery store chicken
jokes.explanation: Good specificity comedy. The exact type and brand of chicken shows how she knows exactly what motivates Bevers.

jokes.text: I looked for his dick forever. All that hair.
jokes.setup: Vet says the sick dog will be fine
jokes.punchline: Ilana reveals she was examining the dog's genitals extensively
jokes.explanation: Characteristically inappropriate Ilana overshare. The casual delivery makes it funnier than pure shock value.

jokes.text: If I wrapped it up in a piece of cheese for him to eat and then I accidentally swallowed that piece of cheese, would I die or be like, super chill?
jokes.setup: Vet offers dog Xanax
jokes.punchline: Ilana devises elaborate scheme to accidentally consume dog medication
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly convoluted logic that's pure Ilana. The 'accidentally' qualifier makes her scheme even funnier.

jokes.text: are human hemorrhoids the same as dog hemorrhoids, and if so, does dog hemorrhoid cream work on human buttholes?
jokes.setup: Ilana continues asking the vet inappropriate questions
jokes.punchline: She asks about cross-species hemorrhoid treatment with clinical specificity
jokes.explanation: Peak Ilana inappropriateness. The clinical phrasing of 'human buttholes' makes it absurdly formal and informal simultaneously.

jokes.text: I'm not your roommate's boyfriend, I'm your husband.
jokes.setup: Bevers is supposed to pretend to be Jeremy
jokes.punchline: He gets confused about which fake relationship he's supposed to have with Abbi
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bevers' confusion and inability to handle simple deceptions. Sets up the scheme's collapse.

jokes.text: You're Jeremy Santos. No, I'm Matty Bevers.
jokes.setup: Abbi desperately tries to maintain the Jeremy deception
jokes.punchline: Bevers immediately reveals his real name, destroying the plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect scheme collapse. Bevers' inability to maintain a simple lie is characteristically frustrating and funny.

jokes.text: Chicken!
jokes.setup: Abbi and Bevers are running from authorities
jokes.punchline: Bevers yells about his promised chicken reward while fleeing
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with maximum economy. One word that recalls the earlier bribe while they're literally running away.

jokes.text: All the dogs are off by one. Oh, my God. The Chihuahua should be the in the loft. Which means you should be in Chinatown, Judith Light.
jokes.setup: Ilana realizes she mixed up all the dog deliveries
jokes.punchline: She directly addresses Judith Light like a real celebrity while explaining the logistics error
jokes.explanation: Great callback maintaining the celebrity bit while revealing a major screwup. Her continued commitment to the name is perfect.

jokes.text: We borrowed neighbor's mail to go get a neighbor's package. You were not a good kisser.
jokes.setup: Abbi lists all the things that went wrong with Bevers' help
jokes.punchline: She includes his bad kissing as one of the crimes/problems
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Abbi's frustration and the personal nature of their fake romantic performance.

jokes.text: Oh, I totally missed, Abbi! Oh!
jokes.setup: Bevers is using Abbi's bathroom
jokes.punchline: He announces his bathroom mishap to her through the door
jokes.explanation: Pure gross-out Bevers character comedy. Not sophisticated but perfectly captures his inappropriate oversharing.

jokes.text: Dude, you gotta know someone you can set me up with. You know what? No, I don't. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Jeremy asks Bevers to set him up with someone
jokes.punchline: Bevers flatly refuses despite living with an available woman
jokes.explanation: Good ironic comedy since Bevers could obviously introduce Jeremy to Abbi but doesn't. Shows his obliviousness or possessiveness.

jokes.text: Am I thin? Like, for a dog?
jokes.setup: Ilana is at the vet with the dog
jokes.punchline: She asks about her own weight using dog standards
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd body image joke. The qualifier 'for a dog' is perfectly Ilana logic - comparative body standards across species.
